headline: From insider trading in France to an American cell phone: How Spain tracked down former prime minister Zapatero
subhead: Judicial cooperation among several countries, including France and Switzerland, has helped uncover an international network relying on 'opaque financial channels,' which were allegedly used, among other things, to pay the former Socialist leader.

headline: Trump and Iran's president sign deal to end the war
European Parliament's approval of new GMOs marks turning point for agriculture sector
Italian historian Carlo Ginzburg, founding figure of microhistory, has died
subhead: Through his research on the 16th and 17th-century Inquisition trials in Europe, he initiated a profound upheaval in the field of historical scholarship. He died on June 17 at the age of 87.

headline: Appointed by Trump to lead the Fed, Kevin Warsh vows to reshape the institution
subhead: On Wednesday, June 17, the US central bank kept its key interest rates unchanged and mentioned a possible hike by the end of the year due to inflation driven by the conflict in the Middle East. Its new chair, who has been critical of indicators used by the institution, launched working groups to reform the Fed.

headline: 2026 World Cup: Cristiano Ronaldo, Luka Modric and Edin Dzeko – still in the game in their forties
subhead: Eight players over the age of 40 are competing in the World Cup, a new record. While most of them are goalkeepers, Portuguese star Cristiano Ronaldo, Croatian midfielder Luka Modric and Bosnian striker Edin Dzeko are notable exceptions. They remain crucial to their teams.

headline: Trump welcomed at Versailles after G7: 'Macron has turned the château into a diplomatic extension of the Elysée'
subhead: In an interview, historian Fabien Oppermann said that the dinner planned for Wednesday highlights the French president's desire 'to impress his counterpart,' in hopes of preserving the transatlantic relationship at a time when ties with the US are especially strained.

'On the ground, it's a disaster': In Bunia, Democratic Republic of Congo, Ebola epidemic spirals out of control
subhead: One month after the Congolese authorities officially declared the outbreak, the health response is failing to contain the virus's spread, and the number of victims continues to rise.
byline: Morgane Le Cam and  Philémon Barbier

headline: Dmitry Muratov, Russian journalist and Nobel laureate: 'I stand with most Russians who do not want war'
subhead: During a visit to France for the International Journalism Festival, Dmitry Muratov, co-founder of the independent Russian newspaper 'Novaya Gazeta,' discusses the dangers facing the media and opposition in Russia.

headline: Weakened at home, Macron counts on 'his' last G7 to boost his diplomatic record
subhead: As his second term grows increasingly complex at home amid the killing of 11-year-old Lyhanna, the French president, who is chairing the G7 summit in Evian, is staking his legitimacy on an ambitious international agenda.

headline: Economic and xenophobic: The double threat posed by the Rassemblement National
'The relationship with Washington that once underpinned Netanyahu's power has become a burden'
'Italy has no need for a Xenia Fedorova, because pro-Russian discourse is relayed through more diffuse and socially accepted channels'
